title: Customizable Time Tracking
excerpt:

posts.author: KevinK (Kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
The ability to pause and continue Time Tracking during certain days/hours.
We Time Track items to have an overview on how long items take before they are processed. To make it fair, time tracking should pause during weekends and after hours. We track anything from 1 Item up to 200 items at a time, so any manual way will not work.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @KevinK - I answered a similar question on this earlier. While it’s not the most perfect/elegant solution, it may do as a workaround.

How to automate timers to stop when it's outside of working hours and business days? Platform discussions
Hey @UFAI - you could utilize some automations to help manage this along with a status & date column to tell the time-tracking to stop (and start if needed). Add a Status column (let’s call it Time Control) with three options: Stop, Start, and “-” (default option), and a Date column (called “Today’s Date”). Then create a few simple automations: When (at 5:00pm for example) “Today’s Date” arrives change “Time Control” to Stop, then change “Time Control” to - and then push “Today’s Date” by 1 d…

Hope this helps,
Mark
